steps.title: Find the main verb
steps.content: The subject is "Louisiana resident Oscar James Dunn." The long phrase between commas only adds description, so the main clause still needs a finite verb to complete what Dunn did in 1868.

steps.title: Reject nonfinite forms
steps.content: Choice A is an infinitive, Choice B is a participial phrase, and Choice C is a present participle; none can serve as the main verb of the clause. The remaining option supplies the needed finite past-tense verb. The answer is D.
